sources.title: Shanmukhapriya Publications
sources.summary: This page details a series of Carnatic music instructional books and song collections published by Shanmukhapriya. Offerings range from foundational exercises for beginners to specialized notations for Jayadeva’s Ashtapadis and Tyagaraja’s devotional songs. The list also previews upcoming volumes dedicated to various varnams and kritis.

kritis.name: Gita Govindam / Ashtapadi
kritis.note: A collection of Jayadeva's lyrical compositions provided with melodic swara notation.

kritis.name: Divyanama Kritis
kritis.note: A series of Tyagaraja's devotional songs intended for group singing and spiritual practice.

sources.title: Hello Everyone
sources.summary: Anuradha Mahesh introduces her personal blog by dedicating her efforts to the divine through a traditional Sanskrit verse. The entry highlights the spiritual importance of light as a symbol of supreme knowledge and the removal of ignorance. This greeting sets a devotional and educational tone for the platform's content.

sources.title: #1-Sangeetha Sampoorna – Beginners Level
sources.summary: An updated third edition of an introductory Carnatic music manual has been released, featuring a wider range of lessons than standard beginner texts. The volume incorporates rare geethams and lakshana geethams, supported by digital audio aids to facilitate learning for both pupils and instructors. It is distributed globally by Shanmukhapriya Publications and includes references to other specialized notation books.

kritis.name: Geethams
kritis.note: The book features rare and classic introductory melodic compositions for beginners.

kritis.name: Lakshana Geethams
kritis.note: These compositions are included to explain the characteristics of various ragas to students.

kritis.name: Gita Govindam/Ashtapadi
kritis.note: A related publication featuring swara notations for these devotional songs is also highlighted.

sources.title: Music and Stress Relief
sources.summary: The text explores how the mind transitions from external stimuli to a meditative internal state during sleep to process unresolved thoughts. It frames this nightly rest as a natural mechanism for achieving psychological order and self-reconnection. This introversion allows the subconscious to express itself freely, away from the distractions of the waking world.

sources.title: Managing Relationships
sources.summary: The author explores the necessity of emotional maturity over mere academic achievement for achieving a balanced life. By harmonizing the heart and the mind, individuals can navigate family dynamics more effectively and find true fulfillment. The piece highlights that spiritual progress is only possible when logic and emotion act as complementary forces.

sources.title: Husband Wife Relationship
sources.summary: This narrative explores the distinction between superficial attachment and profound love through a dialogue between a spiritual teacher and an elderly couple. It uses mythological examples, such as Sri Rama's sacrifice and the differing devotions of Rukmini and Satyabhama, to illustrate the nature of selfless affection. The discourse posits that true love focuses on the well-being of the beloved and brings out the best in an individual.

sources.title: Effect of Saptaswaras and Chakra Meditation
sources.summary: This text examines the relationship between the seven primary swaras and the body's chakra system, highlighting how musical vibrations interact with endocrine glands. It posits that the seventy-two melakarta ragas correspond to specific nerves, offering therapeutic benefits when performed with melodic precision and devotion. The practice emphasizes achieving physical harmony through the alignment of pitch and spiritual focus.

sources.title: Om Namo Narayanaya
sources.summary: This entry highlights the spiritual significance of the Narayana mantra and invites practitioners to share their blessings. It functions primarily as a devotional post centered on the deity Sriman Narayanan.

kritis.name: Om Namo Narayanaya
kritis.note: A sacred eight-syllable mantra that serves as a central theme for many devotional musical works.

sources.title: Auric Clearing
sources.summary: This text describes a spiritual meditation technique aimed at purifying the human energy field of negative influences through visualization. The process involves imagining golden rain that cleanses the aura and physical body while channeling released energy back into the earth. It is presented as a regular exercise to maintain energetic hygiene and personal well-being.

sources.title: Therapeutic Evaluation of the Classical Ragas
sources.summary: This text explores the medicinal applications of various classical ragas, categorizing their benefits according to physical and psychological health. Specific scales are identified as remedies for conditions such as respiratory issues, blood impurities, and emotional imbalances. The analysis highlights how traditional music can serve as a non-invasive tool for enhancing memory and organ function.

sources.title: THINK
sources.summary: This entry shares a brief moral anecdote concerning the conditions of love and the challenges of self-acceptance. It uses a narrative about a visually impaired individual to provoke deeper thought on personal relationships.

sources.title: HAPPY DIWALI
sources.summary: This post conveys festive greetings to the audience in celebration of Diwali. The author expresses heartfelt wishes for prosperity and health during the festival of lights. It focuses on sharing joy and success with the community.

sources.title: Carnatic Music Lessons – Basic
sources.summary: This educational resource offers foundational training materials for newcomers to the Carnatic musical system. It covers essential preliminary exercises and introductory melodic forms to help students build a strong vocal or instrumental base through active listening and repetition.

sources.title: Bhajanam at Chottanikkara Temple – A Divine Experience
sources.summary: The author describes a personal spiritual retreat at the Chottanikkara Bhagavathy temple, focusing on the practice of Bhajanam. This three-day stay provided significant mental clarity and a sense of spiritual rejuvenation.

sources.title: Veena Instrumental By Sri Chitti Babu
sources.summary: This digital resource features a curated collection of instrumental recordings by the legendary veena virtuoso Sri Chitti Babu. It serves as an introductory segment for a series highlighting the performances of various maestros within the Carnatic music tradition. The archive provides listeners with high-quality audio representations of the artist's unique playing style.

sources.title: Anandabhairavi-The Divine Raga
sources.summary: This discussion highlights how the raga evokes feelings of devotion, humility, and supreme happiness in the listener. It is presented as a therapeutic melody capable of calming agitated minds and fostering a sense of peace. The author suggests that incorporating the essence of this ancient raga can lead to a more blissful daily existence.

sources.title: Which is the hardest job in the world?
sources.summary: A candidate for a psychology teaching position is challenged by an unconventional interview question about the world's most difficult job. Despite her experience, she is unable to provide the specific answer sought by the panel. The narrative reflects on the unpredictability of professional evaluations compared to standard academic study.

sources.title: Power of Prayer
sources.summary: The author shares a brief reflection on the spiritual strength found through prayer and the importance of maintaining a positive outlook. The text encourages readers to find reasons for happiness even during challenging times.

sources.title: Sindhubhairavi
sources.summary: This discussion highlights Sindhubhairavi as a vibrant raga with Hindustani origins that bridges the gap between folk and classical styles. It is unique for its inclusion of all sixteen swaras in the Carnatic system, creating a joyful and tranquil atmosphere for the listener. Traditionally recognized as a morning melody in the North Indian system, it is celebrated for its soothing properties.

sources.title: Anuradha Krishnamoorthy – 1st Dec 2007 Vocal Concert
sources.summary: This entry documents a vocal performance by Anuradha Krishnamoorthy from December 2007, providing a tracklist of the featured compositions. The concert includes works by the Trinity and modern composers, highlighting ragas such as Reethigowla and Amruthavarshini. The recording captures essential elements of a live kutcheri, including raga alapana and varied talams.

kritis.name: Thathavamariya
kritis.note: A Papanasam Sivam composition set in the emotive Reethigowla raga and Adi tala.

kritis.name: Sobillu sapthaswara
kritis.note: A vibrant Tyagaraja kriti in Jaganmohini that praises the seven notes of music.

kritis.name: Anandhamrutha
kritis.note: A Muthuswami Dikshitar masterpiece in Amruthavarshini preceded by a raga alapana.

sources.title: Develop Your Singing Voice
sources.summary: This piece explores the human voice as a dynamic instrument capable of significant refinement through intentional practice. It highlights how singers can overcome personal limitations by focusing on technical aspects like pitch, volume, and tonal color. The text underscores the unique nature of vocal expression compared to mechanical instruments.

sources.title: HameerKalyani – A cool breeze from N.India
sources.summary: This raga provides a refreshing melodic experience reminiscent of a cool mountain wind. As a derivative of the 65th Melakarta scale, it blends North Indian influences with traditional Carnatic structures. The post outlines its complex ascending and descending scales which give the raga its unique identity.

sources.title: Sri. Kudamaloor Janardhanan – Pranamam
sources.summary: This post highlights an instrumental album by flautist Kudamaloor Janardhanan titled 'Pranamam.' It features a tribute to Ustad Bismillah Khan and a creative medley of several classical ragas. The tracks demonstrate a blend of melodic patterns through a ragamalika structure.

sources.title: Kudamaloor Janardanan: Ganesha Murali
sources.summary: This page lists track details for 'Ganesha Murali', an instrumental album by flutist Kudamaloor Janardanan. The performance focuses on classical Carnatic compositions dedicated to Lord Ganesha. It serves as a brief discography entry for these specific flute renditions.

kritis.name: Mahaganapathim
kritis.note: A classic invocation in Raga Nattai commonly used to begin Carnatic music concerts.

kritis.name: Sidhi Vinayakam
kritis.note: A revered composition in Raga Shanmukhapriya that honors the deity of wisdom.

sources.title: Sankaran Namboothiri-Paramapurusha Jagadeeswara
sources.summary: This resource provides a tracklist for an album featuring the compositions of Swathi Thirunal as performed by Sankaran Namboothiri. The collection highlights three specific devotional pieces set to traditional ragas and the Adi tala cycle. It serves as a concise reference for listeners exploring the Maharaja's musical contributions.

kritis.name: Paramapurusha
kritis.note: A classic Swathi Thirunal composition set in the lively raga Vasantha.

kritis.name: Parvathinayaka
kritis.note: A melodic piece composed in the contemplative raga Bowli.

kritis.name: Kosalendra
kritis.note: A devotional track rendered in the auspicious raga Madhyamavathi.

sources.title: Brindavana Saranga – A Most Pleasing and Emotional Raga
sources.summary: This raga is a derivative of the Kharaharapriya scale, known for its tranquilizing atmosphere and emotional resonance. There is historical and technical ambiguity regarding its distinction from the raga Brindavani, specifically concerning the precise usage of nishada and gandhara. Key functional notes include rishabham, madhyamam, and nishadam, which define its unique melodic identity.

sources.title: CLAY BALLS
sources.summary: A man discovers a bag of sun-dried clay spheres in a seaside cave and proceeds to absentmindedly throw them into the ocean. This narrative serves as a metaphorical exploration of how people often disregard things of great potential value because they appear plain or unremarkable. The story emphasizes the importance of recognizing the hidden worth in what we encounter.

sources.title: Charulatha Mani- Parthasarathy Swamy Sabha 26th Dec 2010 Concert
sources.summary: This entry lists the repertoire from Charulatha Mani's vocal performance at the Parthasarathy Swamy Sabha in December 2010. The concert featured a selection of traditional compositions ranging from popular invocations to rare vivadi ragas.

kritis.name: Bhajamanasa
kritis.note: A brisk opening composition often attributed to Patnam Subramania Iyer.

kritis.name: Pariyachakama
kritis.note: A rare Tyagaraja kriti set in the fourth Melakarta scale.

kritis.name: O Jagadamba
kritis.note: A classic devotional piece composed in the emotive Anandabhairavi ragam.

sources.title: Life is Beautiful
sources.summary: A young girl seeks closure after the passing of her pet by mailing a letter to the divine. The story highlights a heartwarming moment of human compassion when a postal worker provides a comforting response to the child's message.

sources.title: Improve Communication with your Family
sources.summary: The author reflects on insights from a lecture about family dynamics, highlighting the tendency to show more respect to acquaintances than to one's own children. The post encourages readers to practice the same level of patience and courtesy at home that they would offer to a visiting neighbor.

sources.title: Varnam Lessons
sources.summary: This article highlights the pedagogical significance of Varnams as foundational pieces within the Abhyasa Ganam category. It emphasizes how these compositions serve as the primary vehicle for mastering gamakas and understanding the structural essence of various ragas. Proper training in this form is considered crucial for a student's long-term development and technical proficiency in Carnatic music.

sources.title: Ritigowla – The Raaga with a Mesmerising Melody
sources.summary: Ritigowla is a deeply emotive vakra rakthi raga derived from the Kharaharapriya scale, known for its distinct melodic patterns and meditative quality. It is characterized by its ability to evoke feelings of compassion and devotion, making it a preferred choice for slow-tempo renditions in concerts. This raga is also classified as a bhashanga scale and belongs to the second group of ghana ragas.

sources.title: Sujana VAdlamani-Veena Instumental
sources.summary: This page documents a Veena instrumental performance by artist Sujana Vadlamani. The recital features a selection of classic Saint Tyagaraja compositions across different melodic and rhythmic frameworks. The list provides a snapshot of the specific repertoire chosen for this instrumental session.

kritis.name: Tulasidala
kritis.note: A foundational Tyagaraja composition set in the raga Mayamalavagowla and Rupaka tala.

kritis.name: Vararagalaya
kritis.note: This kriti is a well-known exploration of the raga Chenchu Kamboji in Adi tala.

kritis.name: Paraloka
kritis.note: A profound spiritual composition rendered in the raga Poorvikalyani.

sources.title: Vathapi Tharangam on Various Instruments
sources.summary: This collection features various instrumental interpretations of the iconic opening prayer to Ganesha. It highlights unique performances on the jalatharangam, saxophone, and mandolin by renowned maestros. The compilation demonstrates the versatility of this classic composition across different musical mediums.

kritis.name: Vathapi Ganapathim
kritis.note: A quintessential introductory piece in Carnatic music, performed here on a variety of melodic instruments.

sources.title: Essence of Nidityaasana
sources.summary: The author characterizes Nidityaasana as a practical framework for developing self-confidence when facing life's hardships. It emphasizes seeking internal fortitude rather than relying on the often critical or hollow advice of others. This approach focuses on personal empowerment through meditative contemplation.

sources.title: Godly Vehicles
sources.summary: This article provides a concise reference list of the various vahanas, or animal vehicles, associated with major Hindu deities. It details the symbolic mounts for gods and goddesses such as Ganesha, Saraswathi, and Durga, illustrating their traditional iconographic representations.

sources.title: Kudamaloor Janardhanan-Flute-Madhava Murali
sources.summary: This entry showcases a track list from Kudamaloor Janardhanan's flute album titled Madhava Murali. The collection features popular Carnatic compositions performed on the flute, highlighting various melodic frameworks and rhythmic cycles.

kritis.name: Krishna Nee Begane
kritis.note: A celebrated Kannada composition set to Yamankalyani ragam and Misra Chapu tala.

kritis.name: Alaiypayude En Manam
kritis.note: A famous Tamil piece in Kanada ragam that captures the restless devotion of the mind.

kritis.name: Karuna Cheiyvaan
kritis.note: A soulful prayer in Yadukula Kamboji ragam often associated with the Swathi Thirunal period or Irayimman Thampi.

sources.title: Students from US
sources.summary: Anuradha Mahesh describes her experience teaching a trained Western vocalist from California the fundamentals of Carnatic music. The student demonstrated a swift grasp of the melodic structure while learning a traditional introductory geetham.

kritis.name: Varaveena Mrudupani
kritis.note: A foundational geetham in Mohana raga taught to a student with a background in Western music.

sources.title: Sri Kudamaloor Janardhanan-Gokula Murali
sources.summary: This entry highlights a collection of flute performances by Kudamaloor Janardhanan titled Gokula Murali. The tracks feature popular devotional compositions centered on Krishna and Vishnu, rendered in diverse melodic scales. Each piece is paired with its specific raga and rhythmic cycle.

kritis.name: Sreeman Narayana
kritis.note: A meditative Annamacharya composition presented in the dawn raga Bowli.

kritis.name: Swagatham Krishna
kritis.note: A vibrant invitation to Lord Krishna set in the pentatonic raga Mohanam.

kritis.name: Govardhana Giridhara
kritis.note: A soul-stirring piece by Oothukkadu Venkata Kavi exploring the depths of Darbari Kanada.

sources.title: Happy Vishu to Viewers
sources.summary: This post describes the traditional rituals and cultural significance of Vishu, the Kerala New Year festival. The author highlights customs such as viewing the auspicious Vishukani and the practice of elders gifting money to younger family members to usher in prosperity.

sources.title: The Ganaraga Pancharathna Kritis -Sankaran Namboothiri
sources.summary: This article highlights the spiritual depth and structural brilliance of Saint Tyagaraja's famous Pancharathna compositions. It specifically describes the lyrical themes of the first two gems in this set, which focus on divine praise and the acknowledgment of human flaws. The author emphasizes the composer's lasting impact on the Carnatic musical landscape.

kritis.name: Jagadanandakaraka
kritis.note: This song serves as a poetic compilation of 108 divine names sourced from the Ramayana and Bhagavatha.

kritis.name: Dudukugala
kritis.note: The lyrics outline various human shortcomings and moral failings that result in personal unhappiness.

sources.title: Charukesi- The Raga Close to the Heart
sources.summary: Charukesi is identified as the twenty-sixth melakarta raga, situated within the fifth cycle known as the Bana Chakra. This versatile scale is celebrated for its capacity to express diverse sentiments ranging from romantic longing to peaceful devotion. It is frequently described as having a deeply moving and soulful nature that resonates with the listener's core.

sources.title: A Beautiful letter written by a father to his son. This applies to daughters too
sources.summary: This post features a father's reflective letter to his child, offering guidance on navigating the unpredictable nature of life. The author shares personal wisdom and life lessons intended to help the next generation avoid similar hardships.

sources.title: ChittiBabu-Jamshedpur-1968
sources.summary: This archival listing documents a 1968 veena performance by Chitti Babu in Jamshedpur. The repertoire highlights traditional Carnatic standards, ranging from an opening varnam to a prominent Pancharatna kriti. The recording captures the artist's interpretations of works by the Trinity and other significant composers.

kritis.name: Varnam
kritis.note: A traditional introductory piece composed by Mullaiveedu Rangaswamy Nattuvanar in Nattakurinji.

kritis.name: Vathapi Ganapathim
kritis.note: A classic invocation to Lord Ganesha set in Hamsadhwani raga.

kritis.name: Nada Tanumanisham
kritis.note: A Tyagaraja composition in the raga Chittaranjani that extols the embodiment of music.

kritis.name: Endaro Mahanubhavulu
kritis.note: The famous Sri Ragam Pancharatna kriti expressing salutations to all great souls.

sources.title: V.DoresswamyIyengar – Veena
sources.summary: This recording features the legendary veena maestro V. Doreswamy Iyengar performing two classical compositions. The tracks highlight his expertise in the Mysore style of playing through pieces set in Hamir Kalyani and Bhairavi ragas. The page serves as a brief directory for these specific instrumental renditions.

kritis.name: Venkatasaila Vihara
kritis.note: A devotional piece composed by Subbaraya Shastri in the raga Hamir Kalyani.

kritis.name: Chinthaya Makanda
kritis.note: A major composition by Muthuswami Dikshitar belonging to the Prithvi Linga Kshetra in Bhairavi raga.

sources.title: A parent’s query/worry?
sources.summary: This post explores a parent's concern regarding the emotional sensitivity of children and the difficulty in providing guidance without causing negative reactions. It features advice from Swami Srikantananda on navigating these delicate family dynamics.

sources.title: Jayanthi Kumaresh-Veena
sources.summary: This webpage lists a selection of instrumental tracks performed on the veena by renowned artist Jayanthi Kumaresh. The collection includes a traditional varnam and several classical compositions set in diverse ragas. The focus is on the specific song titles and their respective melodic frameworks.

kritis.name: Arunodayam
kritis.note: A vibrant opening varnam set in the raga Bowli, composed by Dr. M. Balamuralikrishna.

kritis.name: Entha Nee vinaa
kritis.note: A melodic piece in the rare raga Urmika, typically attributed to Patnam Subramania Iyer.

kritis.name: Paalvadiyum Mukham
kritis.note: A famous Tamil composition by Oothukkadu Venkata Kavi that beautifully captures the essence of Natakurinji.

sources.title: Mohanam – The Most Pleasing and Enchanting Raga – Truly “Jaganmohanam
sources.summary: Mohanam is a captivating pentatonic scale classified as an audava-audava janya of the 28th Melakarta, Harikambhoji. Known as Bhoop in the Hindustani tradition, this raga is particularly evocative when performed during the night. Historically, it has been associated with ancient names such as Regupti and Raghupati.

sources.title: Give Time to Your Family
sources.summary: This narrative illustrates the importance of dedicating quality time to elderly parents through a story about a man taking his mother to dinner. It highlights how spouses can support each other in nurturing parental relationships. The piece serves as a poignant reminder to prioritize family connections before time passes.

sources.title: An Interesting Story for All of Us
sources.summary: This narrative contrasts the lifestyle of a content fisherman with the profit-driven advice of a visiting businessman. It emphasizes that true satisfaction often comes from appreciating one's current circumstances rather than pursuing unnecessary growth.

sources.title: SCIENCE OF LIFE MANAGEMENT
sources.summary: This article explores the idea that achieving success in various aspects of life depends on effective management rather than mere luck. It reflects on a discourse regarding how personal and professional achievements are the direct results of organized effort. The text emphasizes that structured life management is a universal requirement for reaching one's goals.

sources.title: Sankaran Namboothiri- Alaipayuthe
sources.summary: This page features a compilation of Carnatic vocal performances by Sankaran Namboothiri. The selection includes well-known compositions by various legendary composers across different ragas and rhythmic cycles.

kritis.name: Alaipayuthey
kritis.note: A rhythmic and emotive composition by Oothukkadu Venkata Kavi set in Raga Kanada.

kritis.name: Ksheera Sagara Sayana
kritis.note: A popular devotional piece by Tyagaraja in the evocative raga Devagandhari.

kritis.name: Radhika Krishna
kritis.note: An Ashtapadi from Jayadeva's Gita Govinda exploring themes of divine love.

sources.title: #9-Shanmukhapriya – The Raga with a Splendiferous Melody
sources.summary: This discussion explores the 56th Melakartha raga, known for its intellectual and spiritual benefits including the enhancement of courage and mental focus. Historically referred to as Chamara in the Dikshitar system, it belongs to the tenth chakra of the musical classification scheme. The melody is celebrated for its ability to energize both the performer and the audience through its connection to higher consciousness.

sources.title: Irish Luck
sources.summary: This narrative recounts a selfless act by a Scottish farmer named Fleming, who rescued a young boy from a dangerous bog. The story highlights themes of compassion and quick action in the face of peril. There is no mention of Carnatic music or related technical elements in this specific passage.

sources.title: Mohana Murali – Kudamaloor Janardhanan
sources.summary: This post highlights select tracks from flautist Kudamaloor Janardhanan's presentation titled Mohana Murali. It features the artist's unique interpretation of a varnam and a piece called Reflections.

kritis.name: Varnam
kritis.note: A personalized version of a traditional melodic form performed by the artist.

kritis.name: Reflections
kritis.note: A musical track included as part of this specific instrumental collection.

sources.title: Written by a 90 year old
sources.summary: A veteran columnist reflects on the wisdom acquired over ninety years, sharing forty-five distinct life lessons. This widely circulated piece offers a philosophical perspective on aging and personal resilience. It is framed as an essential read for those seeking inspiration in their daily lives.

sources.title: Rahesh Vaidhya-Veena- Evergreen Melodies
sources.summary: This entry highlights a series of popular instrumental performances by veena artist Rajesh Vaidhya. The selections focus on classic ragas, presenting a curated set of melodies known for their enduring appeal. It serves as a concise index of the ragas featured in this specific musical presentation.

sources.title: Sudha Raghunathan- Dance of Siva
sources.summary: This collection features a selection of Carnatic compositions performed by Sudha Raghunathan that focus on the cosmic dance of Lord Shiva. The repertoire includes traditional Tamil pieces from various historical periods, each set to distinct melodic frameworks and rhythmic cycles. The tracks highlight the spiritual and aesthetic significance of the deity's movements in Chidambaram.

kritis.name: Idathu Patham
kritis.note: A preparatory devotional verse exploring the divine stance of Shiva.

kritis.name: Adikkondar
kritis.note: A classic Tamil composition depicting the energetic dance of the Lord.

kritis.name: Maniyarangil Nadamadum
kritis.note: This piece describes the deity's presence and performance in the jeweled hall.

kritis.name: Anandha Natamaduvar
kritis.note: A popular song celebrating the blissful dance of the Supreme in Thillai.

sources.title: Amazing Darshan of Lord Hanuman
sources.summary: This post features a series of photographs captured during a spiritual discourse on the Ramayana. The author highlights what is described as a miraculous visual manifestation of Lord Hanuman during the event.

sources.title: Swathi Murali-Kudamaloor Janardhanan
sources.summary: This page outlines a musical track list featuring a collaboration between Swathi Murali and flautist Kudamaloor Janardhanan. The repertoire consists of classical Carnatic compositions set to traditional rhythmic cycles. The featured works are predominantly those of the royal composer Swathi Thirunal.

kritis.name: Gopalaka Pahimam
kritis.note: A devotional composition rendered in the raga Revagupthi and set to Misra Chapu tala.

kritis.name: Parama Purusha
kritis.note: A standard Carnatic piece set in the vibrant raga Vasantha.

kritis.name: Jaya Jaya
kritis.note: A melodic track composed in the raga Manirang and set to Adi tala.

sources.title: Dr.M.Balamuralikrishna – Great Masters Series – Nagumomu
sources.summary: This entry highlights specific tracks from a recording featuring the legendary vocalist Dr. M. Balamuralikrishna. The selection includes a varnam composed by the artist himself as well as a cornerstone work by Tyagaraja. It provides a brief glimpse into the repertoire featured in the Great Masters Series collection.

kritis.name: Amma
kritis.note: A varnam composed by Balamuralikrishna in the raga Gambheeranattai.

kritis.name: Entharo Mahanubavulu
kritis.note: The famous Sreeragam Pancharatna kriti by Saint Tyagaraja.

sources.title: A Nice Article About Love By Swami Vivekananda
sources.summary: The text presents a spiritual metaphor using water to illustrate the delicate nature of human affection. It suggests that love flourishes when held with an open hand rather than through possessive force.

sources.title: Raga Chikitsa: Raga and its Effects
sources.summary: This article explores the therapeutic application of Indian classical music, specifically focusing on the practice of Raga Chikitsa. It describes how musical scales are classified according to the five natural elements to address various health imbalances. The discipline emphasizes the restorative power of melodic structures in achieving holistic well-being.

sources.title: O.S.Arun- Bhakthi Samrajyam-Thyagaraja Krithis
sources.summary: This entry details a selection of tracks from O.S. Arun's album dedicated to the compositions of Saint Thyagaraja. The featured songs span various emotive ragas and traditional rhythmic cycles. It serves as a concise guide to the specific musical arrangements included in the Bhakthi Samrajyam collection.

kritis.name: Ennallu
kritis.note: A meditative piece composed in the scale of Shubhapantuvarali and set to Misra Chapu tala.

kritis.name: Haridaasulu
kritis.note: A devotional composition rendered in the melodic Yamuna Kalyani raga.

kritis.name: Namo Namo Raaghavaaya
kritis.note: This track features a rhythmic salutation to the divine set in the popular Sindhu Bhairavi raga.

sources.title: Maharajapuram Santhanam–Bhajans Of Swami Dayananda Saraswathi
sources.summary: This collection highlights a series of devotional compositions authored by Swami Dayananda Saraswathi and performed by the eminent vocalist Maharajapuram Santhanam. The tracks feature a blend of soulful melodies set to diverse ragas such as Tilang and Ranjani. It serves as a tracklist for an album focusing on spiritual hymns rendered in the Carnatic tradition.

kritis.name: Mahaganapathim
kritis.note: An invocation to Lord Ganesha set in the pleasant raga Tilang.

kritis.name: Ramam Bhaje
kritis.note: A devotional bhajan composed in the raga Durga.

kritis.name: Vandeham Sharadham
kritis.note: A prayerful tribute to Goddess Sharada rendered in Yaman Kalyan.

kritis.name: Dakshina Murthe
kritis.note: A spiritual hymn dedicated to the primordial teacher set in Raga Ranjani.

sources.title: Raga Chikitsa -Its Effects and Timings
sources.summary: The text discusses the traditional practice in Indian music of performing specific ragas at designated times of the day to maximize their aesthetic and therapeutic potential. This temporal alignment suggests that certain melodies achieve their peak beauty only during particular hours, such as dawn or midnight. The concept emphasizes the intrinsic link between musical performance and the natural cycles of time.

sources.title: Maharajapuram Santhanam -Oothukadu Venkata Subbaiyer Songs
sources.summary: This entry catalogs a selection of beloved Oothukadu Venkata Subbaiyer compositions interpreted by the renowned vocalist Maharajapuram Santhanam. The listing focuses on famous devotional pieces dedicated to Lord Krishna, highlighting their respective ragas and rhythmic cycles.

kritis.name: Paal Vadiyum Mugam
kritis.note: A soulful composition in Nattaikurinji describing the radiant face of the infant Krishna.

kritis.name: Alaipayuthe

kritis.name: Thaye Yasoda
kritis.note: Set in Thodi, this kriti portrays the Gopis' complaints to Mother Yasoda about Krishna's mischief.

sources.title: Maharajapuram Santhanam-parthasarathy Swami Sabha 1992-last Public Concert (?)
sources.summary: This tracklist documents a 1992 performance by the legendary vocalist Maharajapuram Santhanam at the Parthasarathy Swami Sabha. The recording is significant as it is noted to be one of the artist's final public appearances. The selection features a blend of traditional compositions and rare melakarta ragas.

kritis.name: Innam enmanam
kritis.note: A melodic varnam in raga Charukesi composed by the violin virtuoso Lalgudi Jayaraman.

kritis.name: Ganamurthe

kritis.name: Anudhinamunu kavumayya

sources.title: Songs on Chottanikkara Bhagavathy – Amme Narayana Devi Narayana
sources.summary: This collection highlights devotional compositions and chants dedicated to the goddess of the Chottanikkara temple. It provides listeners with tracks that focus on the spiritual invocation of the divine mother through sacred music.

kritis.name: Chottanikkara Amme Jagadambike
kritis.note: A song that invokes the blessings of the goddess as the universal mother.

sources.title: #10 – Darbari Kanada – The Emperor of Ragas and the Raga of Emperors.
sources.summary: This raga serves as a cultural bridge, tracing its roots from Southern traditions to the Mughal courts where it was famously performed by Tansen. Its name signifies its historical role in royal settings, embodying a sense of profound authority and philosophical gravity. The scale is widely regarded as a majestic centerpiece of Indian classical music, known for its deep emotional resonance.

sources.title: T.N.Krishnan – Violin
sources.summary: This entry lists a selection of instrumental tracks from a Carnatic violin performance by the renowned musician T.N. Krishnan. The repertoire features foundational compositions from the classical tradition, starting with a traditional Ganesha invocation. The set showcases a variety of moods through well-known ragas and rhythmic cycles.

kritis.name: Mahaganapathim
kritis.note: A standard opening prayer in the vibrant Nata raga dedicated to Lord Ganesha.

kritis.name: Marivere
kritis.note: A profound and devotional piece set in the evocative Anandabhairavi ragam.

kritis.name: Raghuvamsasudha
kritis.note: A lively and technically brilliant composition often used to showcase instrumental dexterity.

sources.title: Distance makes moments of Togetherness a Bliss
sources.summary: This post reflects on the necessity of maintaining individuality and personal space within a partnership to ensure long-term harmony. Drawing from Kahlil Gibran's poetry, it illustrates how independent growth actually strengthens the collective bond between two people. The text uses metaphors of musical strings and architectural pillars to convey the beauty of shared existence without encroachment.

sources.title: Nithyasree – Sree Subramanyomam
sources.summary: This entry documents a selection of recordings by Carnatic vocalist Nithyasree Mahadevan. The compilation includes standard devotional compositions by the Trinity of Carnatic music. The tracks showcase a variety of traditional ragas and rhythmic cycles.

kritis.name: Enneramum

sources.title: Sudha Raghunathan Live Concert on 4/12/2010
sources.summary: This post provides a tracklist for a live performance by vocalist Sudha Raghunathan recorded in late 2010. The selection includes foundational Carnatic forms such as a varnam and a slokam, spanning various popular ragas. The repertoire features compositions from both the Trinity and modern masters.

kritis.name: Devi Niye Tunai

sources.title: Ranjani-Gayathri – Dual Harmony
sources.summary: This page lists a selection of Carnatic vocal tracks performed by the celebrated duo Ranjani and Gayathri. The repertoire primarily features the compositions of Papanasam Sivan, highlighting a mix of popular and specialized ragas. Each entry details the raga, tala, and composer associated with the specific rendition.

sources.title: Sudha Raghunathan-GNB Krithis
sources.summary: This tracklist features a selection of compositions by the legendary G.N. Balasubramaniam as performed by vocalist Sudha Raghunathan. The collection highlights classic GNB krithis set to popular ragas and distinct rhythmic cycles. It serves as a brief catalog of a themed recording focused on the GNB musical lineage.

kritis.note: A vibrant invocatory song composed in the raga Hamsadhwani and set to Adi tala.

kritis.name: Nirathamuka

kritis.name: Sivananda Kaamavardani
kritis.note: A spiritual piece set in the raga Kamavardani utilizing the Misra Chapu rhythmic structure.

sources.title: Don’t Hope…Decide
sources.summary: The author shares a personal epiphany triggered by observing a family reunion at the Portland airport. The narrative suggests that small, unexpected moments can lead to significant shifts in one's perspective on decision-making.

sources.title: An Interview with God
sources.summary: This text explores a fictional conversation with the Divine regarding the puzzling aspects of human existence. It highlights the ironies of how people approach life, growth, and the passage of time. The writing serves as a spiritual reflection rather than a musical analysis.

sources.title: The Star Fish Story
sources.summary: This allegorical tale features a man rescuing individual starfish to demonstrate that small gestures can be life-changing. It serves as a reminder of personal agency and the value of helping others one by one.

sources.title: “Where did you learn?”
sources.summary: During a formal gathering at the Madras Sanskrit College in the late 1950s, the Kanchi Mahaswamigal requested a scholar to complete a Sanskrit verse he had initiated. The account highlights the sage's intellectual depth and his spontaneous interactions with the era's prominent thinkers. This snippet captures a moment of traditional scholarship involving historical figures like Rajaji.

sources.title: The Right Decision
sources.summary: When faced with an unavoidable tragedy, the most rational course of action is to prioritize the survival of the largest number of people. Choosing to divert the train onto the disused track ensures that the total loss of life is minimized, producing the best possible outcome for the collective. This decision-making process emphasizes the importance of maximizing overall welfare in a crisis.

sources.title: THE COFFEE STORY – DON’T MOST OF US DO THE SAME THING?
sources.summary: A group of successful professionals visiting their former mentor find themselves discussing the burdens of their daily lives. The professor uses a variety of coffee cups to illustrate how people often get distracted by superficial containers instead of enjoying the contents of life. The lesson highlights that peace is found in valuing the core essence of existence over external status symbols.

sources.title: M.S.Subbulakshmi-AIR Radio Release-Vol 1
sources.summary: This page originally pertained to a collection of archival radio recordings by the legendary vocalist M.S. Subbulakshmi. However, the primary content has been removed, leaving only the title and social sharing options. No specific musical details or tracklists are currently accessible from the source.

sources.title: Music and Stress Relief
sources.summary: The author describes sleep as a vital period of introspection that allows the mind to process unresolved daily experiences. By retreating from external stimuli, the brain works toward internal order and psychological resolution. This restorative state is presented as a crucial mechanism for reconnecting with one's inner self.

sources.title: Maharajapuram Santhanam Arsha Vidhya Gurukulam_ PA 1991-2
sources.summary: This recording captures a 1991 vocal performance by the legendary Maharajapuram Santhanam at the Arsha Vidhya Gurukulam. The setlist features foundational masterpieces from the Carnatic repertoire, including a Pancharatna kriti and a major composition by Swathi Thirunal. It represents a typical high-energy concert opening sequence characteristic of the artist's popular style.

kritis.name: Vaathaapi Ganapathim
kritis.note: A classic Hamsadhwani invocation often used to begin Carnatic recitals with vibrant energy.

kritis.name: Endharo Mahaanubhaavulu

kritis.name: Narasimha Mamava

sources.title: Purvikalyani – An Aesthetically Melodious, Pleasant and Popular Raga
sources.summary: This janya scale is derived from the fifty-third melakarta and features a unique asymmetrical structure in its melodic ascent and descent. Performing this raga during the early evening hours is traditionally thought to enhance its emotional resonance and beauty. It is historically linked to the name Gamakakriya as documented in classical music texts.

sources.title: Carnatic Vocal – Sankaran Namboothiri
sources.summary: This entry cataloged a vocal performance by Sankaran Namboothiri featuring a selection of classic Carnatic compositions. The repertoire includes well-known works by Saint Tyagaraja and a tribute piece by M.D. Ramanathan.

kritis.name: Marugelara

kritis.name: Jayathi Somagirir

sources.title: Change Your Thinking
sources.summary: This motivational story illustrates the power of perspective through the interaction of two hospital patients. It suggests that our internal outlook can create beauty and hope even in bleak circumstances.

sources.title: Deleting the Copyrighted Albums from this Blog
sources.summary: The blog administrator is removing all downloadable content subject to copyright restrictions to ensure legal compliance. Moving forward, the website will exclusively host live performance recordings from temples and music sabhas. This policy shift prioritizes the sharing of non-commercial archival recordings over studio albums.

sources.title: Helpless Love
sources.summary: This metaphorical narrative personifies human feelings and emotions as they vacation together on a coastal island. The plot shifts toward a crisis when a storm warning forces the personified emotions to evacuate their tropical retreat.

sources.title: Face difficulties positively
sources.summary: The narrative features a parable about a mule trapped in a well to encourage readers to approach life's challenges with a constructive attitude. It emphasizes that internal strength and perspective are vital when external circumstances seem dire.

sources.title: If I happened to die yesterday night
sources.summary: This brief introductory text captures a personal moment of surprise as the author encounters their own photograph in a newspaper during a standard morning routine. It serves as a narrative prelude to a longer reflection regarding daily life and public recognition.

sources.title: #12 – Hamsanadam – Call of a Swan
sources.summary: Hamsanadam is an evocative and bright janya raga known for its romantic character and unique melodic structure. Characterized as a vivadi scale derived from the 60th melakarta, it often surpasses its parent raga in popularity and concert usage. It is traditionally associated with a graceful, swan-like aesthetic and is considered ideal for evening performances.

sources.title: Sudha Raghunathan – Live at Music Academy 1992
sources.summary: This entry documents a live vocal performance by Sudha Raghunathan at the Madras Music Academy in 1992. The featured tracks include a foundational varnam and devotional compositions set to various ragas and talas. The recording captures the artist during a significant era of her concert career.

kritis.name: Ennaganu

sources.title: Beautiful debate
sources.summary: The text features a structured debate between a philosophy professor and a student concerning the existence of God. Through a series of hypothetical questions, the dialogue examines the conflict between empirical science and religious conviction.

sources.title: Simhananda – The longest Thalam in the World (100th Post in this Blog)
sources.summary: Simhanandana is recognized as the most extensive rhythmic cycle in global music, featuring a massive structure of 128 beats. This complex tala utilizes rare rhythmic components such as Guru, Plutha, and Kaakapaada, which are seldom found in standard compositions. Its mathematical depth is evident in the detailed arrangement of eighteen distinct parts and five hundred twelve sub-units.

sources.title: Maharajapuram Santhanam – SSVT 1985
sources.summary: This recording captures a 1985 live performance by the renowned vocalist Maharajapuram Santhanam at the SSVT. The song list features a structured progression from a technical varnam to significant devotional compositions by the Trinity and other major composers. It highlights a selection of traditional Carnatic masterpieces delivered in the artist's signature style.

kritis.name: Sadinchane

sources.title: Tips to Develop Your Singing Voice
sources.summary: The author shares personal insights and practical methods for enhancing vocal clarity and strength. While not a professional voice coach, the writer emphasizes that these experimental approaches have yielded positive results in their own practice. This entry serves as a follow-up to a previous discussion on maintaining a healthy singing voice.

sources.title: #13 – BOWLI – The Meditative Morning Raga
sources.summary: Bowli is a serene morning raga derived from the Mayamalavagowla scale, celebrated for its deeply spiritual and calming influence. Due to its ability to foster emotional stability and focus, it is the primary choice for early morning devotional hymns like Suprabhathams. This raga serves as a musical tool for mental wellness, helping to transition the mind into a peaceful state at dawn.

sources.title: #49-Shodasa Ganapathi Krithis – Muthuswami Deekshithar Compostions
sources.summary: This post highlights the spiritual significance of Ganesh Chaturthi and explains the etymological meaning of the name Ganapathi as a master of intellect. It serves as an introduction to the sixteen iconic compositions dedicated to Lord Ganesha by Muthuswami Dikshitar. The author emphasizes the deity's role as the provider of wisdom and auspiciousness.

sources.title: First Anniversary of My Blog
sources.summary: The author celebrates the first anniversary of her blog, which is dedicated to the global dissemination of Carnatic classical music. She acknowledges the vital role of her readers and the guidance of her teachers in reaching this milestone. The post emphasizes her humble commitment to sharing musical knowledge through her writing.

sources.title: Happy Onam to all Visitors
sources.summary: This post serves as a warm festive greeting to visitors celebrating the harvest festival of Onam. The author shares wishes for joy, prosperity, and light during the holiday season. It is a brief personal message rather than a musical analysis.

sources.title: Upcoming Navarathri Concerts
sources.summary: Anuradha Mahesh details her series of vocal and veena performances scheduled for the 2011 Navarathri festival. These events are set to take place at prominent temples across Kerala, including venues in Thrissur and Kochi. Several recitals will also showcase students from her Shanmukhapriya School of Music.

sources.title: Importance of Varnams
sources.summary: This article discusses the dual identity of Varnams as foundational technical exercises and high-caliber compositions suitable for opening professional performances. It highlights how these pieces bridge the gap between basic musical training and the complexity required for the concert stage.

sources.title: Lemon – kills Cancer Cells!
sources.summary: The provided text discusses the alleged anti-cancer properties of lemons, claiming they are significantly more effective than traditional chemotherapy. It suggests that medical laboratories are withholding this information to prioritize profits from synthetic drugs.

sources.title: Navarathri Concerts 2011- Photographs
sources.summary: This blog post showcases a series of photographs taken during the Navarathri music festival in 2011. The author indicates that multimedia elements, including audio and video recordings of the performances, will be provided in a subsequent update.

sources.title: Raw Fruit and  Tree = Man and his Emotions.
sources.summary: This reflection uses the growth and eventual detachment of a ripening fruit as a metaphor for the human journey through complex emotions. It suggests that while feelings like anger and passion are natural stages of development, one must reflect on these experiences to achieve true spiritual freedom. The goal is to move beyond being a slave to one's impulses to reach a state of inner contentment.

sources.title: Old Age Happiness
sources.summary: The author shares reflections on achieving contentment and peace during the later stages of life. These insights on aging are presented as universally applicable lessons for both retirees and younger generations. The text focuses on the mental shifts required to find happiness in one's senior years.

The tracks highlight the composer's expertise through works set in diverse ragas like Keervani and Mohana Kalyani. It serves as a concise digital catalog of specific devotional musical pieces.

sources.title: Wishing All my friends and visitors a HAPPY DIWALI
sources.summary: This post serves as a brief festive greeting to the website's audience during the celebration of Diwali. It conveys warm wishes for prosperity and joy to all visitors. The entry contains no specific musical analysis or technical content.

sources.title: VOCAL BY VAIKOM VIJAYALEKSHMI
sources.summary: This profile highlights the inspiring journey of Vaikom Vijayalakshmi, a visually impaired musician who has mastered both vocal music and the veena. Despite physical challenges, her artistic dedication is showcased through a curated selection of Carnatic performances. The featured tracks demonstrate her technical proficiency across diverse ragas and rhythmic cycles.

kritis.name: Ohm Kara
kritis.note: A foundational varnam in Raga Shanmukhapriya composed by Balamuralikrishna.

kritis.name: Prasidha Gananadham
kritis.note: A devotional piece dedicated to Ganesha in the raga Preyasi.

kritis.name: Hiranmayi Lakshmim
kritis.note: A classic Mutuswami Dikshitar composition set in the meditative Raga Lalitha.

sources.title: Appreciate Life’s Annoying Little Things
sources.summary: This narrative explores how seemingly frustrating daily delays and minor mishaps actually saved several individuals from the 9/11 terrorist attacks. It encourages readers to view life's small inconveniences with a sense of appreciation and broader perspective.

sources.title: Earthquake and Mother's Sacrifice
sources.summary: A short devotional post sharing an inspirational story about a mother who shielded her child during an earthquake, using her body as protection until she perished. The narrative is framed as a parable about selfless maternal love. It has no direct Carnatic music content.

sources.title: Maha Ganapathy Darshan‏
sources.summary: A devotional post offering a brief Sanskrit prayer to Ganesha and inviting readers to take darshan. The post includes a verse addressed to Ganapathi as remover of obstacles and son of Shiva. No Carnatic music content is present.

sources.title: Nityasree-Live Concert Nov.4th-Fine Arts Society Kochi
sources.summary: A post sharing a track listing from a Nityasree Mahadevan live concert performed at the Fine Arts Society in Kochi. The four pieces include kritis by Muthuswamy Dikshitar, Tyagaraja, Syama Sastri, and Swathi Thirunal across ragas like Naatai, Chamaram, and Bhooshavali. It functions as a recording-share post with minimal commentary.

kritis.name: Siddhivinayakam
kritis.note: Dikshitar composition in Chamaram, Rupaka tala.

kritis.name: Sree Ramapaadama
kritis.note: Tyagaraja composition in Amritavahini, Adi tala.

kritis.name: Paahimaam Sree Rajarajeswari
kritis.note: Syama Sastri composition in Naatai, Rupaka tala.

kritis.name: Gopanandana
kritis.note: Swathi Thirunal composition in Bhooshavali, Adi tala.

sources.title: Sri Gomathi Ambal Krithis – "The Sacred Hymns on Gomathi Ambal, Sankarankoil"
sources.summary: A post sharing three hymns dedicated to Gomathi Ambal of Sankarankoil, with lyrics by Coimbatore Kunjammal set to music and sung by Charulatha Mani. The kritis are rendered in Nattai, Sankarabharanam, and Mohanam ragas. It serves as a recording-share with brief metadata.

kritis.name: Vishnu Sahothariyin
kritis.note: Devotional composition in Nattai raga on Gomathi Ambal.

kritis.name: Innumdayavu
kritis.note: Composition in Sankarabharanam raga praising Gomathi Ambal.

kritis.name: Gomathi Ambalendru
kritis.note: Composition in Mohanam raga on Gomathi Ambal of Sankarankoil.

sources.title: Importance of Thalam / Rhythm in Carnatic Music
sources.summary: A brief introductory article on the role of tala in Carnatic music, comparing rhythmic cycles found in nature to musical rhythm. The post references Tyagaraja's use of the phrase 'sogasugaa mridanga thalamu' to underscore rhythm's centrality. Tala is described as the physical representation of rhythmic cycles in the tradition.

sources.title: Krithis on Chidambaram Natarajar
sources.summary: A track-listing post sharing an audio collection of kritis dedicated to Nataraja of Chidambaram, performed by prominent artists including GNB and M.S. Subbulakshmi. Ragas include Behag, Todi, Kedaram, and Begada, with compositions by Muttu Tandavar and others. The post provides no extended commentary beyond the track list.

kritis.name: Aadum Chidambaramo
kritis.note: Composition in Behag raga on Nataraja of Chidambaram.

kritis.name: Ananda Natesha
kritis.note: Rendered in Todi raga, performed by GNB.

kritis.name: Anandanatanaprakasam
kritis.note: Composition in Kedaram raga, performed by D.K.J.

kritis.name: Chidambaram Enru Orutaram
kritis.note: Composition in Begada raga, performed by GNB.

kritis.name: Chidambarame Ninai
kritis.note: Muttu Tandavar composition in Nada Namakriya raga.

sources.title: #14 – Naatai – The Raga with an Intoxicating Melody
sources.summary: A short appreciation of the raga Nattai, identifying it as a derivative of Chalanata (36th melakarta) belonging to the 6th chakra. The post characterizes Nattai as an auspicious raga traditionally chosen to open concerts, and draws a symbolic link to the heart chakra in a mind-body-soul framework. The text is part of a numbered raga-exploration series by the blogger.

sources.title: Workshop on Voice Training / Voice Culture
sources.summary: A brief announcement about an upcoming voice training and voice culture workshop organised in Kochi, Kerala by the Shanmukhapriya School of Music. No further details about curriculum or instructors beyond Dr. Sak Durga are mentioned. The post is primarily an event notice.

sources.title: Balamurali Krishna-1978 Kalanidhi Concert
sources.summary: A track-listing post for a 1978 Kalanidhi concert recording by M. Balamurali Krishna, featuring a Sama Veda recitation and three kritis in Arabhi, Garudhathvani, and Kapi ragas. One of the tracks is labelled as a self-composition by Balamurali Krishna himself. The post provides no extended analysis.

kritis.name: Sadinchane
kritis.note: Rendered in Arabhi raga at the 1978 Kalanidhi concert.

kritis.name: Thathvameruga
kritis.note: Rendered in Garudhathvani raga.

kritis.name: Bhavame
kritis.note: A self-composition by Balamurali Krishna rendered in Kapi raga.

sources.title: HAPPY NEW YEAR
sources.summary: A brief personal message from the blogger wishing readers a happy and prosperous New Year 2012 with blessings for their families. The post contains no Carnatic music content. It is a seasonal greeting with no musical discussion.

sources.title: Compositions of Syama Sastri – Various Artists
sources.summary: A track-sharing post listing four Syama Sastri compositions rendered by different vocalists including T.M. Krishna, D.K. Jayaraman, Mysore Nagamani Srinath, and R. Vedavalli. Ragas featured include Yadukulakambhoji, Lalitha, and Abheri. The post provides minimal contextual information beyond artist and raga attributions.

kritis.name: Amba Kamakshi
kritis.note: Rendered in Yadukulakambhoji raga by T.M. Krishna.

kritis.name: Tharunam Eedamma
kritis.note: Rendered by D.K. Jayaraman, raga unspecified in excerpt.

kritis.name: Nannubrova
kritis.note: Rendered in Lalitha raga by Mysore Nagamani Srinath.

kritis.name: Ninnuvina Marigalada
kritis.note: Rendered in Abheri raga by R. Vedavalli.

sources.title: The Art of Singing Ragaalapana
sources.summary: An introductory explanation of ragalapana (raga alapana), describing it as a slow rhythmless improvisation that exposes the character and mood of a raga through permitted swaras and characteristic phrases. The post explains that alapana syllables such as 'tha dha ri na' are used instead of spelling out note names, and defines core concepts like arohana and avarohana. It is an educational primer aimed at lay listeners.

sources.title: HAPPY PONGAL GREETINGS TO ALL VISITORS
sources.summary: A short festive greeting to blog visitors on the occasion of the Tamil harvest festival Pongal, wishing them joy and prosperity. No Carnatic music content is present. The post is a seasonal personal message only.

sources.title: Thrissur Brothers – Navarathri Concert, Changambuzha Park, 15-10-2010
sources.summary: A track-listing post for a Navarathri concert by the Thrissur Brothers at Changambuzha Park, performed in October 2010. Three pieces are listed: a varnam in Nattakurinji, a composition in Begada, and a piece in Poornachandrika. The post is a recording share with no analytical commentary.

kritis.note: Varnam rendered in Nattakurinji raga.

kritis.name: Vallabha Nayakasya
kritis.note: Composition rendered in Begada raga.

kritis.name: Telisi Rama
kritis.note: Composition rendered in Poornachandrika raga.
